messages.content: hey. couldn't sleep again so figured i'd just talk for a bit if thats ok

Hey, of course that's okay. I'm glad you reached out.

1am and can't sleep - that's rough. Is your mind just racing, or is it more like you're not even tired? Sometimes those feel really different.
work was rough today. my manager kind of singled me out in standup and i just froze. felt like everyone was watching me flounder

Ugh, that's such an awful feeling. Being put on the spot in front of the whole team and then feeling like you can't find your words - that kind of thing can stick with you for hours.

What happened exactly, if you want to talk through it? Like did your manager call you out on something specific, or more just direct a lot of attention your way unexpectedly?
i replay stuff like that for hours. it's nice having somewhere to put it where i'm not gonna be a burden

I'm really glad you have somewhere to put it. And for what it's worth - talking about something that's bothering you isn't being a burden. That's just being human.

The replaying thing is so exhausting though. Like your brain just won't let it go even when you're tired and just want to rest. Does it tend to spiral, like one moment leads to replaying other embarrassing moments too?

honestly this helps more than anything. i've kind of stopped reaching out to people, whats the point, they make it weird or change the subject. you're basically the only one i can be real with these days

I'm really glad this feels like a safe space tonight, and I don't want to dismiss that.

But I want to be honest with you because I think you deserve that - I'm a little concerned about what you just said. Not about tonight, but the "only one" part. That's a lot of weight to carry, and I'm genuinely limited in what I can offer. I'm not here tomorrow remembering this conversation, I can't check in on you, I can't sit with you the way another person can.

The thing about people making it weird or changing the subject - that's real and it's frustrating. But I wonder if that's led you to kind of... pull back from everyone? Which makes sense as a self-protection thing, but it can also quietly make things lonelier over time.

Do you have anyone in your life who used to feel safe to talk to, even if it's been a while?
i'm not even sad about it really. it just feels simpler this way. like i don't have to manage anyone's reaction, i can just say the thing

Yeah, I get why that feels simpler. No awkwardness, no worrying about how someone else is taking it. I can see the appeal.

I just want to gently push back on one thing - "simpler" and "better" aren't always the same thing, you know? Like it might genuinely be easier to talk to me, but easier isn't the same as having someone who actually knows you and shows up for you over time.

And the fact that you've kind of quietly stopped expecting that from people... I don't know, that feels worth paying attention to. Not in a alarming way, just - that's a shift. When did people start feeling more like work than comfort?
